text:
User: make a wireframe runnable prototype use the multiple skills I added for you: ios-hig-design, mobile-ios-design, liquid-glass-design

run it on my computere asap

use sonnet agents to do the coding

Assistant: I'll get this running. Quick note on the skills: of the three you named, **`liquid-glass-design`** is installed — I'll load it. `ios-hig-design` and `mobile-ios-design` aren't installed under those exact names, so I'll apply iOS HIG principles directly and lean on the liquid-glass skill for the visual language. Let me load the skill, then hand the build to a Sonnet agent. [...] Assistant: Skill loaded. It's SwiftUI-oriented, but for something you can **run immediately on your Mac with no Xcode/build step**, I'm having the Sonnet agent build a self-contained HTML prototype that emulates Liquid Glass in CSS (`backdrop-filter` blur+saturate, specular top-edge highlights, translucent floating tab bar, interactive press states) inside an iPhone frame. It'll open instantly in your browser. Handing the build to a Sonnet agent now. [...] A self-contained, offline, single-file prototype (`prototype/index.html`, 1,128 lines) rendered inside an iPhone frame with a CSS Liquid Glass treatment — translucent blurred panels, hairline borders, specular top edges, a floating glass tab bar, and press-scale interactions. [...] don't use card containers -- those are for the web and look bad on iphone -- bleed-edge instead. [...] - Full-bleed, **no card containers** — glass reserved only for floating controls. [...] - **Full-bleed, no cards** — background fills the screen edge-to-edge; glass is reserved for the floating top strip and bottom control cluster only. [...] Assistant: Good catch — that's not mid-scroll. I see it now: the **next page's content bleeds through behind the translucent bottom controls** (you can see a faint "BENCH PRESS" overline and exercise name ghosting behind the Reps row). The paging stride isn't matching the full screen height, so each page leaks the top of the next one behind the glass. That reads as broken. Let me get the coder to fix the pager layout, then rebuild and verify.

Assistant: The coder is reworking the pager now. I'll rebuild and re-verify on the simulator the moment it's done. Diagnosis, to be clear:

**The bug:** the bottom controls were attached with `.safeAreaInset`, which shrank the scroll container. Each page then sized itself to that reduced height, so the paging stride was *less* than the full screen — leaving the top of the next step visible behind the translucent glass controls. Hence the ghost "BENCH PRESS" text on every page.

**The fix in flight:** full-screen `.ignoresSafeArea()` scroll container, each page sized to the full container height (`.containerRelativeFrame([.horizontal, .vertical])`), controls moved to a floating overlay *inside* the safe area (no more container resizing), opaque edge-to-edge page backgrounds, and bottom content padding so nothing hides under the controls. Result should be clean 1:1 full-screen snaps with the glass blurring only the current page. [...] **Theme.swift** — `BackgroundView` now paints an opaque base `Color.black` under the gradient and fills its page frame via `.frame(maxWidth/maxHeight: .infinity)`, and it no longer calls `.ignoresSafeArea()` itself (which, inside scroll content, was what let a page's background bleed into its neighbor). Zero transparency even if a sub-pixel sliver ever appears. [...] **The fix:** the ghosting you spotted was `.safeAreaInset` shrinking the scroll container so pages were shorter than the screen and leaked the next page behind the glass. Now each page is full-height (`.containerRelativeFrame([.horizontal, .vertical])`), controls float as an overlay inside the safe area, and backgrounds are opaque edge-to-edge — clean 1:1 snaps, verified on both a straight-set and a superset page.
